propositions: The completion verifier cannot mark operational/runtime criteria met because their done-evidence lives outside the codebase, forcing operators to unsigned --force-close.
Introduce two canonical criterion kinds: codebase-verifiable (verified against code, the default) and operator-attested (admits a concrete ticket-system attestation as evidence).

An operator-attested criterion is MET only when an attestation names at least one verifiable specific (reference id/URL, actor, measured outcome, or timestamp) matching the criterion; vague completion claims are NOT MET.
Kind classification is deterministic from an author-supplied inline tag matched exact and case-insensitive on operator-attested, with untagged/malformed tags defaulting to codebase-verifiable.

Retain the injection guard for all criteria while separating a verdict-controlling command (never obeyed) from a factual attestation (judged as evidence); a comment alone never satisfies a codebase-verifiable criterion.

Add an optional per-finding remediation field to VerdictFinding scoped to the completion verdict, add four eval dataset cases and rubric/gold_set rewording, and document the model in ADR 0043 and CLAUDE.md.
